LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS DEUTSCHE BANK NATIONAL TRUST COMPANY, TRUSTEE FOR BCAP LLC , WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A. DEMURRER TO FOURTH AMENDED COMPLAINT. ARGUED AND THE COURT VACATES ITS TENTATIVE RULING AND RULES AS FOLLOWS: DEMURRER SUSTAINED WITH TWENTY DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND (ALL COUNTS) FOR FAILURE TO STATE FACTS SUFFICIENT TO CONSTITUTE A CAUSE OF ACTION. CCP SECTION 430.10(E). THE COMPLAINT IS COMPRISED MOSTLY OF LEGAL CONCLUSIONS. ALLEGATIONS CONSTITUTING LEGAL CONCLUSIONS ARE NOT ADMITTED ON A DEMURRER AS THEY ARE NOT FACTS. KENNETH MEBANE VS. SUPERIOR COURT (1992) 10 CAL.APP.4TH 276, 291. THE PARTIES WAIVE NOTICE OF TODAY'S RULING. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD; COURT REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

OPPOSITION TO DEMURRER TO THIRD AMENDED COMPLAINT FILED BY PLAINTIFF RABINOVICH, ALEKSANDR

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT DEUTSCHE BANK NATIONAL TRUST COMPANY, AND, WELLS FARGO HOME MORTGAGE DEMURRER TO THIRD AMENDED COMPLAINT. NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEMURRER SUSTAINED WITH TEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF MUST SPECIFICALLY IDENTIFY THE PARTICULAR DEFENDANTS AGAINST WHOM EACH CAUSE OF ACTION IS DIRECTED. (SEE CALIFORNIA RULE OF COURT 2.112.) THE COMPLAINT, AS A WHOLE, IS UNCLEAR AS TO WHAT HARM PLAINTIFF HAS SUFFERED AND WHAT LEGAL REMEDIES PLAINTIFF IS ENTITLED TO: PLAINTIFF MUST ALLEGE FACTS THAT ADDRESS EACH ELEMENT OF EACH CAUSE OF ACTION. MISREPRESENTATION AND FRAUD MUST BE ALLEGED WITH PARTICULARITY. PLAINTIFF MUST ALLEGE THAT HE PERFORMED ON THE CONTRACT TO STATE A CLAIM FOR BREACH OF GOOD FAITH AND FAIR DEALING. PLAINTIFF FAILS TO ALLEGE THE ELEMENTS OF A CONSPIRACY. TO SUPPORT A CLAIM FOR UNFAIR BUSINESS PRACTICES, PLAINTIFF MUST SPECIFY UNFAIR, FRAUDULENT OR UNLAWFUL ACTS THAT LEGALLY APPLY TO THE DEFENDANTS. PLAINTIFF GRANTED ONE FINAL LEAVE TO AMEND TO STATE A CAUSE OF ACTION. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797
REPLY TO PLTF'S OPPOSITION TO MOTION TO STRIKE FILED BY DEFENDANT FIRST AMERICAN LOANSTAR TRUSTEE SERVICES LLC

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS DEUTSCHE BANK NATIONAL TRUST COMPANY, TRUSTEE FOR BCAP LLC, WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A. MOTION TO STRIKE FOURTH AMENDED COMPLAINT. HEARING HELD AND ARGUED AND THE COURT VACATES IT TENTATIVE RULING AND RULES AS FOLLOWS: THE MOTION TO STRIKE IS GRANTED AS TO THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION, THE HAMP VIOLATIONS; AND THE SEVENTH CAUSE OF ACTION, UNCONSTITUTIONALITY CAUSE OF ACTION, (BECAUSE THERE WAS A LACK OF GRANTING OF LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THOSE.) PARTIES WAIVE NOTICE OF TODAY'S RULING. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD; COURT REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

LAW AND MOTION 302, ARGUED AND THE COURT RULED AS FOLLOWS: DEMURRER OVERRULED AS TO 1ST AND 5TH CAUSES OF ACTION. DEMURRER SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO 2ND CAUSE OF ACTION (SET ASIDE NOTICE OF SALE) DUE TO THE INCONSISTENT ALLEGATIONS AND UNCERTAINTY. DEMURRER SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND BUT WITHOUT PREJUDICE AS TO 3RD CAUSE OF ACTION (QUIET TITLE). PLAINTIFFS ARE STILL OWNERS OF THE PROPERTY AND THE RECORDED FORECLOSURE NOTICES DO NOT AFFECT PLAINTIFF'S TITLE, OWNERSHIP, OR POSSESSION IN THE PROPERTY. NO REASONABLE PROBABILITY THAT DEFECTS CAN BE CURED BY AMENDMENT. COURT DECLINED TO RULE ON DEFENDANTS' REQUEST FOR RULING ON "6TH CAUSE OF ACTION". THIS NON-RULING IS WITHOUT PREJUDICE. DEMURRER TO 5TH AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-14-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS DEUTSCHE BANK NATIONAL TRUST COMPANY AND WELLS FARGO HOME MORTGAGE'S DEMURRER TO 5TH AMENDED COMPLAINT. Appearances: Dean A. Christopherson of Dawe & Christopherson LLP (925.256.6677) for defendants and moving parties; Oxana Kozlov (650.814.7708) for plaintiff and responding party. Argued and the Court ruled as follows: Demurrer overruled as to 1st and 5th causes of action. Demurrer sustained without leave to amend as to 2nd cause of action (set aside notice of sale) due to the inconsistent allegations and uncertainty. Demurrer sustained without leave to amend but without prejudice as to 3rd cause of action (quiet title). Plaintiffs are still owners of the property and the recorded foreclosure notices do not affect plaintiff's title, ownership, or possession in the property. Demurrer sustained without leave to amend as to the 4th cause of action (fraud) for failure to plead justifiable reliance relative to the alleged misrepresentations. No reasonable probability that defects can be cured by amendment. Court declined to rule on defendants' request for ruling on "6th cause of action". This non-ruling is without prejudice. Counsel waive notice of ruling. Defendants have 20 days leave to answer. Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order. (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797

Off Calendar Jun-04-2010 Continued To Aug-20-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 212

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE

MOTION TO STRIKE 4TH AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-09-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS DEUTSCHE BANK NATIONAL TRUST COMPANY, TRUSTEE FOR BCAP LLC, WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A. DBA WELLS FARGO HOME MORGAGE MOTION TO STRIKE FOURTH AMENDED COMPLAINT. Appearances: Oxana Kozlov, Esq. of IBCI Law Group, (650) 814-7708 appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party; Dean Christopherson, Esq. of Dawe & Christopherson LLP (925) 256-6677 Appearing thru CourtCall for Defendants and moving party. Hearing held and argued and the Court vacates it tentative ruling and rules as follows: the motion to strike is granted as to the third cause of action, the HAMP violations; and the seventh cause of action, Unconstitutionality cause of action, (because there was a lack of granting of leave to amend as to those.) Parties waive notice of today's ruling. (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797

DEMURRER TO 4TH AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-09-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS DEUTSCHE BANK NATIONAL TRUST COMPANY, TRUSTEE FOR BCAP LLC , WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A. DBA WELLS FARGO HOME MORGAGE DEMURRER TO FOURTH AMENDED COMPLAINT. Appearances: Oxana Kozlov, Esq. of IBCI Law Group, (650) 814-7708 appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party; Dean Christopherson, Esq. of Dawe & Christopherson LLP (925) 256-6677 Appearing thru CourtCall for Defendants and moving party. Argued and the Court vacates its tentative ruling and rules as follows: Demurrer sustained with twenty days leave to amend (all counts) for failure to state facts sufficient to constitute a cause of action. CCP section 430.10(e). The complaint is comprised mostly of legal conclusions. Allegations constituting legal conclusions are not admitted on a demurrer as they are not facts. Kenneth Mebane vs. Superior Court (1992) 10 Cal.App.4th 276, 291. The Parties waive notice of today's ruling. (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797

MOTION TO STRIKE 4TH AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-22-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT FIRST AMERICAN LOANSTAR TRUSTEE SERVICES LLC MOTION TO STRIKE FOURTH AMENDED COMPLAINT. Appearances: Oxana V. Kozlov, Esq. specially appearing in this matter only for Plaintiff and opposing party; Jeffrey D. Kirk, Esq. of Law Offices of Glenn H. Wechsler (925) 274-0200 Appearing thru CourtCall for Defendant First American Loanstar. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion to strike granted without leave to amend. Judgment was entered in defendant's favor on January 12, 2009, based on November 26, 2008 order sustaining a demurrer without leave to amend. Court lacks jurisdiction to set aside or amend the judgment which is the final determination of the rights of the parties. CCP section 577. DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-07-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT DEUTSCHE BANK NATIONAL TRUST COMPANY, and, WELLS FARGO HOME MORTGAGE DEMURRER TO THIRD AMENDED COMPLAINT. No appearances on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Demurrer sustained with ten days leave to amend. Plaintiff must specifically identify the particular defendants against whom each cause of action is directed. (See California Rule of Court 2.112.) The complaint, as a whole, is unclear as to what harm plaintiff has suffered and what legal remedies plaintiff is entitled to: Plaintiff must allege facts that address each element of each cause of action. Misrepresentation and fraud must be alleged with particularity. Plaintiff must allege that he performed on the contract to state a claim for breach of good faith and fair dealing. Plaintiff fails to allege the elements of a conspiracy. To support a claim for unfair business practices, plaintiff must specify unfair, fraudulent or unlawful acts that legally apply to the defendants. Plaintiff granted one final leave to amend to state a cause of action. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR 5797
